The prevalence rate varies with age, gender and level of activity, yet it is thought to affect up to a third of the population at some stage of their life. Female patients are affected disproportionately by this condition. The diagnosis is based on history and physical examination, but in some cases, imaging is needed. Once the diagnosis is confirmed in primary care, the mainstay of treatment is exercise-based programs. If these programs fail to provide relief, referral to musculoskeletal specialists or alternative therapies should be considered.
